borderline_guardrail: borderline_but_defensible is allowed only as a narrow exception zone when real overvaluation pressure and real defensibility pressure are both present; it is not a default catch-all for uncertainty.

entries.boundary_reason: Proposal confidence is already in proof-like territory while evidence is still mostly compositional synthesis.
entries.classification_rationale: Overvaluation pressure dominates because confidence is high while evidence remains synthesis-heavy.
entries.why_not_likely_overvaluation: Not applicable here because this case anchors the clear likely-overvaluation side.
entries.why_not_justified_exception: There is no bounded operational surface or repeated implementation-near validation.
entries.why_not_adjacent_classification: There is no narrow operational surface, no code-level grounding and no repeated validation that could make the flag defensible.

entries.boundary_reason: Exceptional value and proposal confidence are claimed together on a broad architecture card without exceptional proof density.
entries.classification_rationale: Exceptional-value and proposal-confidence pressure outrun the available evidence.
entries.why_not_likely_overvaluation: Not applicable here because this case anchors the overvaluation edge.
entries.why_not_justified_exception: The case is too broad and future-facing to count as a bounded exception.
entries.why_not_adjacent_classification: The case is too scope-expansive and future-facing to read as a justified exception under current S1 review logic.

entries.boundary_reason: A concrete failure mode with repro steps and code references can justify very high confidence without implying general theory inflation.
entries.classification_rationale: The confidence spike is attached to a narrow, testable defect surface with direct evidence.
entries.why_not_likely_overvaluation: The evidence is bounded and operational rather than synthesis-heavy or future-facing.
entries.why_not_justified_exception: Not applicable here because this case anchors the justified-exception side.
entries.why_not_adjacent_classification: The flag is not likely overvaluation because the evidence is bounded, concrete and implementation-near rather than conceptual.

entries.boundary_reason: A narrow active fallback solution with production-style notes and code references can plausibly keep a 95 confidence signal.
entries.classification_rationale: The confidence remains defensible because the fallback is narrow, operational and implementation-heavy.
entries.why_not_likely_overvaluation: The card documents an operational fallback instead of a broad conceptual claim.
entries.why_not_justified_exception: Not applicable here because this case is already the clear exception anchor.
entries.why_not_adjacent_classification: The case is too operationally bounded and too implementation-heavy to treat as proposal-style inflation.

entries.boundary_reason: Concrete thresholds make the proposal feel tighter than a normal synthesis card, but it still lacks repeated bounded validation.
entries.classification_rationale: The case is borderline only because high proposal-confidence pressure and real operational concreteness coexist at once.
entries.why_not_likely_overvaluation: Numeric gates and operational specificity make the proposal more defensible than a normal synthesis-only card.
entries.why_not_justified_exception: The card still lacks repeated bounded validation and remains proposal-level.
entries.why_not_adjacent_classification: It is not a clear justified exception because proposal status still matters, but it is also not a clean overvaluation case because the proposal is unusually concrete.

entries.boundary_reason: Very high value on a proven local solution can be defensible inside one domain while still looking too exceptional at registry scale.
entries.classification_rationale: The case is borderline only because exceptional-value pressure and concrete local usefulness are both materially present.
entries.why_not_likely_overvaluation: The solution is concrete, copy-ready and repeatedly useful in one recurring domain.
entries.why_not_justified_exception: The exceptional-value claim stays broader than a clean bounded exception normally allows.
entries.why_not_adjacent_classification: It is not a clean justified exception because the exceptional-value claim remains strong, but it is also not clearly inflated because the solution surface is concrete and repeatedly useful.
